main_paper.raw: {"methods":["ARTICULATE ANYTHING pipeline: automated conversion of any rigid 3D mesh into an articulated object in an open-vocabulary manner","Movable Part Segmentation using 2D segmentation masks (SAM) lifted to 3D, with instance-level semantic labeling via GPT-4o","Articulation Estimation using geometric clues and visual prompting with GPT-4o to infer joint parameters without reliance on training datasets","Refinement stage using 2D diffusion models and Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) loss, with a novel optimization strategy that applies random transformations of movable parts during optimization to preserve part semantics"],"problems":["Limited ability of existing methods to model a wide range of articulated objects in an open-vocabulary context","Dependence on limited, handcrafted articulated object categories in training data","Lack of methods for automatic generation of diverse, realistic articulated 3D objects with accurate part semantics and joint parameters","Insufficient scale and diversity in existing articulated object datasets for training generalizable models"],"datasets":["Objaverse (Deitke et al., 2024)","PartNet-Mobility (Xiang et al., categories)","value":"Matches state-of-the-art articulated object generation methods for selected categories"},{"metric":"Generalization to broader object categories","value":"Demonstrates capacity to open-vocabulary manner, not limited by training categories","Novel articulation parameter estimation method leveraging geometric clues and visual prompting with GPT-4o, enabling open-vocabulary joint estimation","Novel optimization strategy for diffusion-based refinement that preserves part semantics by applying random transformations of movable parts during SDS loss optimization","Ability to generate large-scale, high-quality 3D articulated objects across diverse categories, significantly expanding the coverage of existing datasets"]}

methods: ARTICULATE ANYTHING pipeline: automated conversion of any rigid 3D mesh into an articulated object in an open-vocabulary manner
Movable Part Segmentation using 2D segmentation masks (SAM) lifted to 3D, with instance-level semantic labeling via GPT-4o
Articulation Estimation using geometric clues and visual prompting with GPT-4o to infer joint parameters without reliance on training datasets

Refinement stage using 2D diffusion models and Score Distillation Sampling (SDS) loss, with a novel optimization strategy that applies random transformations of movable parts during optimization to preserve part semantics
problems: Limited ability of existing methods to model a wide range of articulated objects in an open-vocabulary context
Dependence on limited, handcrafted articulated object categories in training data
Lack of methods for automatic generation of diverse, realistic articulated 3D objects with accurate part semantics and joint parameters
Insufficient scale and diversity in existing articulated object datasets for training generalizable models

results.value: Matches state-of-the-art articulated object generation methods for selected categories
Demonstrates capacity to generate a broader range of articulated objects beyond categories present in PartNet-Mobility

novelty_claims: First pipeline to convert any rigid 3D mesh into an articulated object in an open-vocabulary manner, not limited by training categories
Novel articulation parameter estimation method leveraging geometric clues and visual prompting with GPT-4o, enabling open-vocabulary joint estimation
Novel optimization strategy for diffusion-based refinement that preserves part semantics by applying random transformations of movable parts during SDS loss optimization
Ability to generate large-scale, high-quality 3D articulated objects across diverse categories, significantly expanding the coverage of existing datasets

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Ditto: an implicit neural representation-based model for joint part-level geometry and articulation model estimation from interactive perception","Use of ConvONets for learning local implicit fields from convolutional feature grids","Encoding partial point clouds before and after interaction with PointNet++","Feature fusion with a self-attention layer to combine clouds","Establishing correspondences between partial observations before and after object interaction","Enabling digital twins suitable for physical simulation and embodied AI methods: Ditto: an implicit neural representation-based model for joint part-level geometry and articulation model estimation from interactive perception
Use of ConvONets for learning local implicit fields from convolutional feature grids
Encoding partial point clouds before and after interaction with PointNet++
Feature fusion with a self-attention layer to combine information from before/after interaction
Implicit occupancy decoder for geometry reconstruction
Implicit segmentation decoder for part segmentation
Implicit decoders for dense prediction of relative joint parameters for articulation estimation
End-to-end training of all implicit decoders
problems: Automated creation of interactive digital twins of real-world articulated objects for virtual environments
Joint estimation of part-level geometry and articulation models from visual observations
Category-agnostic reconstruction of articulated objects from partial point clouds
Establishing correspondences between partial observations before and after object interaction
Enabling digital twins suitable for physical simulation and embodied AI applications
metrics: Part-level geometry reconstruction accuracy (e.g., IoU, Chamfer Distance)
Articulation model estimation accuracy (e.g., joint parameter error)

results.value: Ditto achieves superior IoU compared to baselines on both PartNet-Mobility and SAPIEN datasets (exact values not specified in provided text)
Ditto outperforms baselines on both datasets (exact values not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: First method to jointly reconstruct part-level geometry and articulation model of articulated objects from interactive perception using implicit neural representations
Category-agnostic approach that works for unknown object categories
Dense, local prediction of joint parameters for robust articulation estimation, as opposed to global prediction
Digital twins produced are suitable for physical simulation and can be directly used in embodied AI and AR/VR applications
End-to-end trainable framework that integrates geometry, segmentation, and articulation estimation

Incorporation of part-level features into object NeRF models
Use of SAM (Segment Anything Model) for oversegmentation
Use of CLIP for 2D dense pixel-level feature embedding

novelty_claims: First approach to build open-vocabulary object-level NeRFs with fine-grained (part-level) understanding
Proposes a two-stage mask clustering method for consistent instance segmentation across frames

methods: Real2Code pipeline: reconstructs articulated objects via code generation from visual observations
Part-level segmentation using a fine-tuned SAM (Segment Anything Model)
3D shape completion model for reconstructing part geometry
Extraction of oriented bounding boxes (OBBs) from reconstructed parts as input abstraction
Fine-tuned large language model (LLM) for predicting joint articulation as code (Python simulation code)
Incorporation of DUSt3R dense stereo reconstruction model for view-consistent 3D segmentation from multi-view RGB images
problems: Reconstructing articulated objects from unstructured RGB images
Generalizing articulated object reconstruction to objects with more parts and complex kinematics than seen in training
Improving the quality and usability of part reconstructions and articulation parameters for simulation
Overcoming the lack of depth and camera information in real-world environments

results.metric: Maximum number of articulated parts reliably reconstructed

results.value: Significantly outperforms prior state of the art (exact numbers not provided)

novelty_claims: First approach to formulate articulated object reconstruction as a code generation problem using LLMs, enabling arbitrary structural complexity without model architecture changes.
First method to accurately reconstruct objects with more than three articulated parts, up to 10 parts, generalizing beyond training set complexity.
Introduces the use of oriented bounding boxes (OBBs) as an abstraction layer for LLM input, improving joint prediction from geometry.
Combines pre-trained vision and language models for scalable and generalizable articulated object reconstruction.
Demonstrates generalization from synthetic training data to real-world objects using only a handful of pose-free RGB images, without depth or camera information.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Definition and identification of 9 Generalizable and Actionable Part (GAPart) classes across 27 object categories","Construction of the GAPartNet dataset with part-level semantic and pose annotations","Domain-generalizable 3D part segmentation method using domain adversarial learning (domain-invariant feature learning with a domain classifier)","Part-oriented feature query technique for context invariance","Multi-resolution technique to handle diverse part sizes","Focal loss to address imbalanced part distribution","Pipeline for part segmentation, part pose estimation, and part-based object manipulation leveraging GAPart definitions and heuristics"],"problems":["Cross-category generalizable object perception and manipulation","Domain generalization for 3D part segmentation and pose estimation across seen and unseen object categories","Robust part-based object manipulation in both simulation and real world","Handling context, size, and distribution variations of parts across object categories"],"datasets":["GAPartNet (constructed in this work, based on PartNet-Mobility and AKB-48)"],"metrics":["AP50 (Average Precision at IoU 0.5) for part segmentation"],"results":[{"metric":"AP50 on seen object categories","value":"76.5%"},{"metric":"AP50 on unseen object categories","value":"37.2%"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of the concept of Generalizable and Actionable Parts (GAParts) for cross-category object perception and manipulation","Creation of GAPartNet, a large-scale, part-centric interactive dataset with rich part semantics and pose annotations","First pipeline for domain-generalizable 3D part segmentation and pose estimation via domain-invariant feature learning","Integration of part-oriented feature query, multi-resolution, and focal loss techniques for robust domain generalization","Demonstration of generalizable part-based object manipulation policies that require minimal human effort for new categories"]}

methods: Definition and identification of 9 Generalizable and Actionable Part (GAPart) classes across 27 object categories
Construction of the GAPartNet dataset with part-level semantic and pose annotations
Domain-generalizable 3D part segmentation method using domain adversarial learning (domain-invariant feature learning with a domain classifier)
Part-oriented feature query technique for context invariance
Multi-resolution technique to handle diverse part sizes
Focal loss to address imbalanced part distribution
Pipeline for part segmentation, part pose estimation, and part-based object manipulation leveraging GAPart definitions and heuristics
problems: Cross-category generalizable object perception and manipulation

Robust part-based object manipulation in both simulation and real world
Handling context, size, and distribution variations of parts across object categories
datasets: GAPartNet (constructed in this work, based on PartNet-Mobility and AKB-48)
metrics: AP50 (Average Precision at IoU 0.5) for part segmentation
novelty_claims: Introduction of the concept of Generalizable and Actionable Parts (GAParts) for cross-category object perception and manipulation
Creation of GAPartNet, a large-scale, part-centric interactive dataset with rich part semantics and pose annotations
First pipeline for domain-generalizable 3D part segmentation and pose estimation via domain-invariant feature learning
Integration of part-oriented feature query, multi-resolution, and focal loss techniques for robust domain generalization
Demonstration of generalizable part-based object manipulation policies that require minimal human effort for new categories

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Kinematic-aware prompting framework for LLMs","Unified kinematic knowledge parser (textual representation of kinematic joints and contact locations)","Kinematic-aware planner (generates 3D manipulation waypoints via chain-of-thought prompting)","Hierarchical prompting: first generating abstract manipulation sequence, then 3D waypoints"],"problems":["Generalizable articulated object manipulation across diverse and unseen objects","Reducing data requirements for robotic manipulation learning","Bridging the gap between high-level reasoning and low-level robotic control using LLMs"],"datasets":["48 object instances across 16 categories in Isaac Gym simulator","7 real-world object categories (for real-world experiments)"],"metrics":["Success rate (implied from context, as typical in manipulation tasks)"],"results":[{"metric":"Zero-shot success on unseen categories (Isaac Gym)","value":"Powerful zero-shot capability for 8 unseen articulated object categories with only 17 demonstrations"},{"metric":"Performance on seen categories (Isaac Gym)","value":"Outperforms traditional methods on 8 seen categories"},{"metric":"Real-world generalization","value":"Demonstrated adaptability on 7 real-world object categories"}],"novelty_claims":["First to propose a kinematic-aware prompting framework that leverages LLMs for low-level articulated object manipulation.","Unified kinematic knowledge parser for representing diverse articulated objects as textual descriptions for LLM prompting.","Kinematic-aware chain-of-thought prompting to generate precise 3D manipulation waypoints from LLMs.","Demonstrates zero-shot generalization to unseen articulated object categories with minimal demonstrations.","Shows real-world applicability and generalization of the proposed framework."]}

methods: Kinematic-aware prompting framework for LLMs
Unified kinematic knowledge parser (textual representation of kinematic joints and contact locations)
Kinematic-aware planner (generates 3D manipulation waypoints via chain-of-thought prompting)
Hierarchical prompting: first generating abstract manipulation sequence, then 3D waypoints
problems: Generalizable articulated object manipulation across diverse and unseen objects
Reducing data requirements for robotic manipulation learning
Bridging the gap between high-level reasoning and low-level robotic control using LLMs
datasets: 48 object instances across 16 categories in Isaac Gym simulator
metrics: Success rate (implied from context, as typical in manipulation tasks)

results.metric: Zero-shot success on unseen categories (Isaac Gym)
results.value: Powerful zero-shot capability for 8 unseen articulated object categories with only 17 demonstrations
Outperforms traditional methods on 8 seen categories
Demonstrated adaptability on 7 real-world object categories

novelty_claims: First to propose a kinematic-aware prompting framework that leverages LLMs for low-level articulated object manipulation.
Unified kinematic knowledge parser for representing diverse articulated objects as textual descriptions for LLM prompting.
Kinematic-aware chain-of-thought prompting to generate precise 3D manipulation waypoints from LLMs.
Demonstrates zero-shot generalization to unseen articulated object categories with minimal demonstrations.
Shows real-world applicability and generalization of the proposed framework.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Structure from Action (SfA) framework","Sequential interaction policy for exposing hidden part geometry and kinematics","Dynamic part reconstruction module for segmenting and completing object parts via aggregated visual observations","Joint estimation module for inferring joint types and parameters from observed motion"],"problems":["Discovering 3D part geometry and joint parameters of unseen articulated objects","Generalizing articulated object structure discovery to categories not seen during training","Segmenting multiple parts and reconstructing part geometry under occlusion","Inferring all joint parameters in a canonical coordinate frame","Handling cross-category generalization for articulated object structure discovery"],"datasets":["PartNet-Mobility"],"metrics":["3D IoU (Intersection over Union)","Joint estimation accuracy"],"results":[{"metric":"3D IoU (unseen categories)","value":"SfA outperforms state-of-the-art pipeline by 25.4 percentage points"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of Structure from Action (SfA), a framework that jointly learns interaction and perception for 3D articulated object structure discovery","SfA learns a sequential interaction policy to expose hidden part geometry and kinematics, rather than relying on random or pre-defined interactions","SfA aggregates visual observations in 3D to improve part segmentation and joint estimation, enabling persistent part tracking even under occlusion","A single SfA model trained in simulation generalizes to many unseen object categories and real-world objects, relaxing prior assumptions about object pose and interaction availability","SfA achieves significant improvement over state-of-the-art pipelines on unseen categories"]}

methods: Structure from Action (SfA) framework
Sequential interaction policy for exposing hidden part geometry and kinematics
Dynamic part reconstruction module for segmenting and completing object parts via aggregated visual observations
Joint estimation module for inferring joint types and parameters from observed motion
problems: Discovering 3D part geometry and joint parameters of unseen articulated objects
Generalizing articulated object structure discovery to categories not seen during training
Segmenting multiple parts and reconstructing part geometry under occlusion
Inferring all joint parameters in a canonical coordinate frame
Handling cross-category generalization for articulated object structure discovery

results.value: SfA outperforms state-of-the-art pipeline by 25.4 percentage points

novelty_claims: Introduction of Structure from Action (SfA), a framework that jointly learns interaction and perception for 3D articulated object structure discovery
SfA learns a sequential interaction policy to expose hidden part geometry and kinematics, rather than relying on random or pre-defined interactions
SfA aggregates visual observations in 3D to improve part segmentation and joint estimation, enabling persistent part tracking even under occlusion
A single SfA model trained in simulation generalizes to many unseen object categories and real-world objects, relaxing prior assumptions about object pose and interaction availability
SfA achieves significant improvement over state-of-the-art pipelines on unseen categories

Introduce few-shot prompt tuning for part name embeddings to improve GLIP's performance in 3D segmentation
Develop a multi-view feature aggregation module for better global understanding of 3D shapes
Propose the PartNet-Ensembled (PartNetE) benchmark for low-shot and text-driven 3D part segmentation evaluation

methods: AdaAfford framework: learns to perform very few test-time interactions to adapt manipulation affordance priors to instance-specific posteriors for 3D articulated objects
Data-efficient strategy for sequentially sampling uncertain or interesting locations for interaction based on current affordance predictions and past interaction trials
Fast-adaptation mechanism that incorporates interaction outcomes (location, direction, resulting part motion) to update affordance predictions
problems: Accurately perceiving and interacting with 3D articulated objects (e.g., cabinets, doors, faucets) in human environments
Learning manipulation affordance that accounts for hidden kinematic constraints (joint location, limits) and dynamic factors (friction, restitution) that are not observable from passive visual inputs
Reducing uncertainty in manipulation affordance predictions for novel 3D articulated objects with limited interaction data
datasets: PartNet-Mobility dataset (972 shapes from 15 object categories)
metrics: Quantitative evaluation of affordance adaptation accuracy (e.g., improvement in affordance prediction after few-shot interactions)
Number of interactions required for effective adaptation

results.metric: Number of interactions for effective adaptation
results.value: as few as one to four interactions needed to adapt affordance to novel test shapes
AdaAfford outperforms baselines (e.g., Where2Act) in quantitative evaluation on PartNet-Mobility

novelty_claims: AdaAfford is the first framework to address hidden kinematic and dynamic uncertainties in manipulation affordance prediction by learning to perform few-shot test-time interactions.
Proposes a data-efficient, fast-adaptation mechanism that updates affordance predictions based on real interaction outcomes, rather than relying solely on passive observations.
Establishes a large-scale benchmark for evaluating manipulation affordance adaptation using PartNet-Mobility and SAPIEN, demonstrating superior performance over prior methods.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["OPTIMUS: Offline Pretrained TAMP Imitation System (a system for training visuomotor Transformer policies via imitation learning)","Large-scale data generation using Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) for supervision","Training high-frequency (30-50Hz) low-level visuomotor Transformer policies by imitating TAMP agents"],"problems":["Scaling up robot manipulation policy learning without labor-intensive human supervision","Generating large, diverse, and relevant datasets for robot learning efficiently","Imitating Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) with closed-loop, reactive visuomotor policies","Addressing challenges in learning from TAMP demonstrations, including privileged information and multimodal data"],"datasets":["Large-scale TAMP-generated demonstration datasets (over 300 long-horizon manipulation tasks, 72 different objects)"],"metrics":["Success rate"],"results":[{"metric":"Success rate","value":"70-80% on a wide variety of challenging vision-based manipulation tasks"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of OPTIMUS, a novel framework for training high-frequency visuomotor Transformer policies by imitating TAMP agents","A new data-generation pipeline specifically curated for imitation learning from TAMP","Demonstration that large Transformer policies trained on TAMP data can outperform TAMP itself in some cases","Extensive study of the design decisions and challenges in imitating TAMP, including high-frequency end-to-end visuomotor control, task plan adaptation, and scene generalization"]}

methods: Grounding DINO: an open-set object detector combining the DINO Transformer-based detector with grounded pre-training
Tight fusion of language and vision modalities at three phases: feature enhancer (neck), language-guided query selection (query initialization), and cross-modality decoder (head)
Feature enhancer using self-attention, text-to-image cross-attention, and image-to-text cross-attention in the neck module
Language-guided query selection for initializing detection head queries
Cross-modality decoder with image and text cross-attention layers in the head phase
Large-scale grounded pre-training using sub-sentence level text features to improve region-text alignment
problems: Open-set object detection: detecting arbitrary objects specified by human language inputs (category names or referring expressions)
Generalization to novel/unseen object categories in object detection
Effective fusion of language and vision modalities in object detectors
Zero-shot transfer in object detection (detecting objects without training data from the target dataset)
Referring expression comprehension: detecting objects described with attributes
novelty_claims: Grounding DINO is the first to tightly fuse language and vision modalities at all three phases (neck, query initialization, head) in a Transformer-based detector for open-set object detection.
Proposes a feature enhancer, language-guided query selection, and cross-modality decoder for effective cross-modality fusion.
Introduces sub-sentence level text features for grounded pre-training, improving region-text alignment over previous sentence-level approaches.
Evaluates open-set object detection not only on novel categories but also on referring expression comprehension with attributes.
Achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on COCO and ODinW benchmarks.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["PartDistill: a cross-modal distillation framework for 3D shape part segmentation","Teacher-student model: 2D vision-language model (VLM) as teacher, 3D point cloud network as student","Bi-directional distillation: forward distillation (2D to 3D) and backward distillation (improving 2D predictions using 3D student feedback)","Utilization of generative models to create additional 3D shapes for knowledge distillation"],"problems":["Zero-shot 3D shape part segmentation","Transferring 2D VLM predictions to 3D segmentation, especially in regions not visible in 2D projections","Inconsistency among 2D predictions from VLMs in multi-view images","Lack of knowledge accumulation across different 3D shapes in previous methods"],"datasets":["ShapeNetPart","PartNetE"],"metrics":["mIoU (mean Intersection over Union)"],"results":[{"metric":"mIoU (ShapeNetPart)","value":"more than 15% higher than previous methods"},{"metric":"mIoU (PartNetE)","value":"more than 12% higher than previous methods"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of PartDistill, a cross-modal distillation framework transferring 2D VLM knowledge to 3D part segmentation","Bi-directional distillation mechanism that not only transfers knowledge from 2D to 3D but also refines 2D predictions using 3D student feedback","Ability to generalize to both bounding-box and pixel-wise VLM predictions","Capability to leverage generative models to create additional 3D shapes for distillation","Significant improvement over existing methods on benchmark datasets in both zero-shot and few-shot settings"]}

methods: PartDistill: a cross-modal distillation framework for 3D shape part segmentation
Teacher-student model: 2D vision-language model (VLM) as teacher, 3D point cloud network as student
Bi-directional distillation: forward distillation (2D to 3D) and backward distillation (improving 2D predictions using 3D student feedback)
Utilization of generative models to create additional 3D shapes for knowledge distillation
problems: Zero-shot 3D shape part segmentation
Transferring 2D VLM predictions to 3D segmentation, especially in regions not visible in 2D projections
Inconsistency among 2D predictions from VLMs in multi-view images
Lack of knowledge accumulation across different 3D shapes in previous methods

results.value: more than 15% higher than previous methods
more than 12% higher than previous methods

novelty_claims: Introduction of PartDistill, a cross-modal distillation framework transferring 2D VLM knowledge to 3D part segmentation
Bi-directional distillation mechanism that not only transfers knowledge from 2D to 3D but also refines 2D predictions using 3D student feedback
Ability to generalize to both bounding-box and pixel-wise VLM predictions
Capability to leverage generative models to create additional 3D shapes for distillation
Significant improvement over existing methods on benchmark datasets in both zero-shot and few-shot settings

Localization-Aware Region-Text Contrastive Loss for training object detectors with generated region-text pairs

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Coarse-to-fine affordance learning pipeline for articulated object manipulation","Two-stage approach: (1) Coarse affordance prediction on noisy far-view point cloud to localize manipulation area, (2) Fine affordance prediction on less noisy near-view point cloud for precise action proposal","Integration of features from both coarse (global) and fine (local) point clouds for action proposal","Use of an eye-on-hand camera to obtain adaptive, task-specific views","Adaptation of PointNet++ to encode both far and near point cloud data"],"problems":["Robust manipulation of 3D articulated objects in the presence of noisy real-world point cloud data","Sim-to-real gap: performance degradation when transferring models trained on perfect simulated point clouds to noisy real-world scans","Loss of important geometric features (e.g., handles, edges) in noisy point clouds, hindering fine-grained manipulation"],"datasets":["Large-scale simulated noisy point clouds mimicking real-world scans","Real-world point cloud data captured with an eye-on-hand camera"],"metrics":["Affordance prediction accuracy","Manipulation success rate"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First to propose a coarse-to-fine affordance learning framework specifically to mitigate the effect of point cloud noise in articulated object manipulation","Leverages the property that point cloud noise decreases as the camera moves closer, using an eye-on-hand camera to obtain adaptive views","Integrates global (coarse) and local (fine) geometric information for robust action proposal under noisy conditions","Adapts PointNet++ to encode and fuse features from both far and near point clouds for improved affordance learning"]}

methods: Coarse-to-fine affordance learning pipeline for articulated object manipulation

Two-stage approach: (1) Coarse affordance prediction on noisy far-view point cloud to localize manipulation area, (2) Fine affordance prediction on less noisy near-view point cloud for precise action proposal
Integration of features from both coarse (global) and fine (local) point clouds for action proposal
Use of an eye-on-hand camera to obtain adaptive, task-specific views
Adaptation of PointNet++ to encode both far and near point cloud data
problems: Robust manipulation of 3D articulated objects in the presence of noisy real-world point cloud data
Sim-to-real gap: performance degradation when transferring models trained on perfect simulated point clouds to noisy real-world scans
Loss of important geometric features (e.g., handles, edges) in noisy point clouds, hindering fine-grained manipulation
datasets: Large-scale simulated noisy point clouds mimicking real-world scans
Real-world point cloud data captured with an eye-on-hand camera
novelty_claims: First to propose a coarse-to-fine affordance learning framework specifically to mitigate the effect of point cloud noise in articulated object manipulation
Leverages the property that point cloud noise decreases as the camera moves closer, using an eye-on-hand camera to obtain adaptive views
Integrates global (coarse) and local (fine) geometric information for robust action proposal under noisy conditions
Adapts PointNet++ to encode and fuse features from both far and near point clouds for improved affordance learning
